title: Peak detection engine + probability scoring
acceptance_criteria: PeakDetectionEngine class subscribes to straddle.values Redis stream using consumer group 'peak-detection'/'primary'
On each snapshot, computes: expansion_pct = (straddle_value - open_straddle_value) / open_straddle_value × 100 where open_straddle_value = first snapshot after 9:15 AM
Computes ROC = (straddle[t] - straddle[t-1]) / straddle[t-1] and acceleration = roc[t] - roc[t-1]
Maintains EMA(8min, 20min) windows over the snapshot history

Detects exhaustion when: expansion_pct >= SIGNAL_MIN_EXPANSION_PCT (env, default 10%), acceleration < SIGNAL_ACCELERATION_THRESHOLD (env, default -0.5), ROC has declined for >= SIGNAL_ROC_DECLINE_CANDLES (env, default 3) consecutive snapshots, SIGNAL_CONFIRMATION_CANDLES (env, default 2) confirming bars seen
Computes raw_exhaustion_score (unclamped float) as a weighted combination of expansion_pct, depth of ROC decline, and acceleration magnitude

Before publishing a signal, calls getMacroContext(redis) from T-65 to fetch global macro context (US VIX, S&P 500, DAX, crude oil, gold) from Redis. If getMacroContext throws or returns all-null, proceeds with null MacroContext (macro factors treated as unavailable — no adjustment applied).

Calls scoreProbability(input) from T-23 with { rawExhaustionScore, signalType:'MOMENTUM_EXHAUSTION', indiaVix: currentVix, macro: macroContext, signalTimeMs: now, clock }. The full expanded probability model (7 factors) is applied automatically.

Stores raw_exhaustion_score (unclamped, from peak detection math) AND adjusted_probability (from scoreProbability) AND adjustmentBreakdown as a JSON string in a new straddle_signals.adjustment_breakdown TEXT column (add to migration 003)
Assigns confidence_tier: 'HIGH' if adjusted_probability >= 0.70, 'MEDIUM' if >= 0.50, 'LOW' otherwise
Signal deduplication: no new MOMENTUM_EXHAUSTION signal published within 300 seconds of the previous one (per underlying)
Writes to straddle_signals table (must exist from migration 003 — task T-25 runs first)
Publishes to Redis signals.generated stream as: {signal_type:'MOMENTUM_EXHAUSTION', signal_id, underlying, atm_strike, spot, straddle_value, vix, adjusted_probability, confidence_tier, signal_time}

Unit tests: given straddle snapshot sequences with known expansion/ROC/acceleration values, exhaustion_score must be computed correctly; probability adjustments are bounded and clamp correctly; dedup window prevents double-firing
PeakDetectionConfig interface exported with all thresholds as configurable fields (loaded from env vars)
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
